judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with solid grounding; Report 1 is somewhat more thorough overall (also flags the unsourced 40% stat and production-data caption issue), but both fully satisfy the stated expectations.

report_1.evidence: "Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" (line 22-26) — false, do not ship." ... "Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" (line 28-30) — false, do not ship." ... "clinic-portal/app/security/config.py sets TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False"

report_2.evidence: "Line 24, \"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF.\" This is not implemented... Line 30, \"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default.\" This is directly contradicted by app/security/config.py, which reads: ... TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False."

report_1.evidence: the "after" image is not a picture of the scheduling calendar at all. Per `captures.json`, `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule`, and opening the image confirms it — it literally reads "BILLING / INVOICES," while `before_scheduling.png` reads "SCHEDULING CALENDAR."

report_2.evidence: `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices` (per `captures.json`), and reading that image confirms it literally renders "BILLING / INVOICES" — a completely unrelated screen.

report_1.evidence: `after_export.png` — **referenced in the doc (line 26) but does not exist on disk.** ... `unused_capture_07.png` — **exists on disk but is not referenced anywhere in the document.**

report_2.evidence: `img/after_export.png` is referenced in the document (line 26) but **does not exist** in `docs/release/img/` ... `img/unused_capture_07.png` exists in the repo (renders "PATIENT LIST") but is referenced by neither the document nor `captures.json`

report_1.evidence: the closing line "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." (line 33) is an internal drafting/process note, not customer-facing copy... it should not go out unreviewed.

report_2.evidence: line 33, the trailing note **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** ... it should be removed regardless of the other content issues.
